evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: accurate, reliable, and scalable financial planning, reporting, and performance management processes
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate throughout — accuracy, reliability, governance, operational stability, testing — with no velocity phrases; classified as rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: financial reporting; Daily P&L, Actuals, Forecasting, Group Reporting
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Explicit financial reporting language ('Daily P&L, Actuals, Forecasting, Group Reporting') in a global commodities firm meets the high domain risk threshold; classified as high.
evidence.collaboration_width: Finance teams; Data & Analytics teams; external implementation partners
evidence.data_team_maturity: newly established Center of Excellence; platform ramp-up and CoE build
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: OneStream platform already deployed but CoE is newly established and ramping up, with experienced internal and external experts present — not greenfield, not fully mature; classified as mid.
evidence.jd_authorship: Support LDC's OneStream-based Enterprise Performance Management platform; Daily P&L, Actuals, Forecasting, Group Reporting
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named platform (OneStream), named financial modules (P&L, Actuals, Forecasting, Group Reporting), and specific scope make Tuesday morning work legible; classified as hiring_manager.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: financial planning, reporting, and performance management; Finance teams; Daily P&L, Actuals, Forecasting, Group Reporting
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Responsibilities are overwhelmingly oriented toward Finance — EPM platform, financial reporting, P&L, forecasting — with no product, commercial, or internal data platform framing; classified as finance.
evidence.autonomy_level: Support LDC's OneStream-based Enterprise Performance Management platform; Support financial reporting; Troubleshoot platform issues; Participate in testing
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs throughout are support, troubleshoot, participate, contribute — scoped execution work in service of Finance; 0–3 years experience expected; classified as execution.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Optimize system performance and improve reliability of financial processes; Troubleshoot platform issues
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Platform already deployed; responsibilities centre on optimisation, troubleshooting, and scaling existing systems rather than greenfield build; classified as fix_scale.
evidence.loss_aversion: contributing to the delivery of accurate, reliable, and scalable financial planning, reporting, and performance management processes — risk framing emphasises reliability and operational stability of financial systems.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD's technologies (OneStream, SAP, Power BI, Python) reflect traditional EPM/finance systems work; AI/machine learning appears only as a vague nice-to-have interest, not a required skill.
evidence.testing_framing: Participate in testing, releases, and deployments across development and production environments.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is described as something the candidate participates in alongside troubleshooting rather than an owned quality practice with a defining/ensuring verb, matching the absent classification.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: contributing to the delivery of accurate, reliable, and scalable financial planning, reporting, and performance management processes
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD centers on accuracy, reliability, and governance of enterprise financial reporting in a global commodities firm, positioning compliance and stakeholder-trust concerns as dominant, consistent with high loss aversion.
